message: docs: clarify descriptions on search and image demos

files.patch:
@@ -10,8 +10,9 @@

TurboQuant Image Similarity

-

Click any image to find similar ones. Description embeddings compressed 6.2x with TurboQuant.

-

1,000 Unsplash photos. Similarity computed via tq.dot() directly on compressed 384-dim vectors.

+

Click any image to find similar ones. The entire image index is just 0.2 MB (compressed from 1.5 MB).

+

1,000 Unsplash photos. Each image is represented by a 384-dim embedding vector, compressed 6x with TurboQuant. + Similarity is computed directly on the compressed vectors — no decompression needed.

@@ -10,10 +10,9 @@

TurboQuant Vector Search

-

Compress embeddings. Search client-side. No server needed.

-

Dot product computed directly on compressed vectors via WASM — no decompression step.

-

TurboQuant is lossy (~3 bits/dim). Top results match, but borderline results near the ranking cutoff - may swap due to small score differences (~0.02). This is the expected tradeoff: 6x less memory for ~80-100% recall.

+

Search 5K Wikipedia passages entirely in your browser. The search index is 1.2 MB instead of 7.7 MB.

+

Left: TurboQuant searches compressed vectors directly (no decompression). Right: brute-force on full float32 vectors. + Top results match. Borderline results near the cutoff may swap — that's the tradeoff for 6x less data.
